concept: Day / One Light-Dark Cycle / Period of Waking
description: The period of light between sunrise and sunset; a complete cycle of light and dark. 'Philo' names the fundamental unit of lived time—the rhythm of waking and sleeping, working and resting.
sound_symbolism: The word opens to light. 'Phi' /ɸi/ begins with gentle breath, dawn arriving. Then 'lo' flows through the hours, the day unfolding.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'ta philo' (one day). Numerals attach directly—never 'lo' with a number. The basic unit for measuring lived time, alongside 'phaeno' (week), 'lunoa' (month), and 'torua' (year).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Day-aligned living reduces energy use—matching human activity to solar availability.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The day was life's natural unit—work with light, rest with dark, each day complete in itself.
